intro: Migrating from C# to Kotlin moves systems programming from the .NET ecosystem to the managed Java Virtual Machine (JVM) or multi-platform runtime layers. While sharing deeply equivalent modern object paradigms and execution concepts, developers trade .NET specific primitives like struct value types and LINQ extensions for lightweight async coroutines and sound nullability controls. This migration guarantees top-tier developer speed across enterprise microservices and mobile engines.

diffs.from: Static typing utilizing reified generic type metadata and optional nullable warning annotations.
diffs.to: Static typing utilizing type-erased generics, built-in null-safe parameters, and smart type casting mechanics.

diffs.from: C-style brace syntax using explicit property get/set pathways, file namespaces, and mandatory semicolons.
diffs.to: Expressive minimal structure eliminating semicolons, providing data class shortcuts, and using trailing closure expressions.

diffs.from: .NET execution engine optimizing memory via stack-based value structures (structs) and dynamic heap garbage collector runs.
diffs.to: JVM managed layer utilizing object tracing garbage collectors, optimizing class instances dynamically via escape analyses.

diffs.from: Task Parallel Library running async operations across thread pools using language-level async/await tools.
diffs.to: Lightweight Coroutines running concurrent tasks sequentially via suspending functions and asynchronous structural channels.

diffs.from: Unified enterprise library providing robust LINQ database interfaces and deep system utilities.
diffs.to: Rich collection extensions framework focusing heavily on functional pipelines and asynchronous data flows.

examples.title: Data Record Representation
examples.description: Compares standard C# structural records against concise Kotlin data class definitions containing built-in utility functions.
examples.fromCode: public record Employee(string Name, int Id);
examples.toCode: data class Employee(val name: String, val id: Int)

examples.title: Automatic Smart Type Casting
examples.description: Demonstrates C# manual type pattern checking against Kotlin's automatic smart-casting capability.

tips.title: Transition Tasks to Coroutines
tips.body: Replace expensive C# Task thread scheduling operations with Kotlin coroutines. Coroutines are incredibly lightweight, non-blocking processes that let you execute thousands of concurrent tasks on few system threads.

tips.title: Master Type Erasure Differences
tips.body: Kotlin erases generic type parameters during compilation due to JVM constraints. Use inline functions equipped with the `reified` keyword to access generic types at runtime, mimicking C# reified generics.

tips.title: Leverage Built-in Extension Functions
tips.body: C# uses static classes for extension methods. Kotlin supports extension functions natively; you can add new methods to any class directly inside any file without creating boilerplate wrapper classes.

tips.title: Ditch Structs for Inline Classes
tips.body: Kotlin lacks direct equivalents to C# stack structs. To avoid heap allocation overhead for single wrapper values, use Kotlin `value class` modifiers to inline data structures directly at compile time.
